questions.question: A production line has stopped because raw material is unavailable. Which department should take immediate action?
During final inspection, a batch is rejected due to quality issues. Which department should coordinate with Production to investigate the problem?
A machine requires preventive maintenance during the production schedule. Which two departments should coordinate first?
Finished goods have passed quality inspection and are waiting for customer shipment. Which department is responsible for arranging transportation?
Management wants to reduce manufacturing costs without affecting quality. Which departments should work together most closely?
A new employee is joining the Quality department. Which department manages the joining process?
Inventory records show fewer raw materials than physically available. Which department should investigate inventory accuracy?
Which department is primarily responsible for ensuring machines remain available for production?
Which department verifies supplier performance before approving future purchases?
Customer complaints about defective products should first be reviewed by which department?
Production and Quality departments must work together to maintain product standards.
Dispatch can release products without verifying shipment documentation.
Warehouse operations directly affect production continuity.
Preventive maintenance helps reduce unexpected machine breakdowns.
HR is responsible for approving production schedules.
Finance provides information that supports business decision-making.
Procurement only purchases materials and has no role in supplier management.
Quality inspections help improve customer satisfaction.
Why is communication between Production and Procurement important?
What could happen if Quality inspections are skipped before dispatch?
How does preventive maintenance improve production efficiency?
Why should Warehouse inventory records always be accurate?
How does Finance support operational departments?
Name any two departments that work closely with Production and explain why.
Explain why coordination between all departments is essential for successful manufacturing operations.
